mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 10.0 percent, it signals a labor market inflection that reduces recession risk premiums, causing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic acceleration that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or the cross-sectional IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances reflect improving consumer liquidity and spending capacity, which directly supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when RSAFS declines, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during high-volatility market regimes (VIX > 30).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month annualized inflation rate exceeding 2.5 percent forces market participants to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the forward return distribution shows no statistically significant negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls serves as a leading indicator of aggregate demand expansion, which systematically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between payroll growth and forward SPX returns falls below 0.10, or the signal generates negative alpha in a long-short portfolio construction.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real GDP growth and persistently low federal funds rates creates a liquidity-driven risk-on environment that amplifies equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the signal underperforms a single-factor GDP model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a recessionary contraction signals collapsing corporate capacity utilization and margin compression, which mechanically drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the forward return distribution does not exhibit a statistically significant negative mean.
